trial_label: Tamoxifen Citrate, Letrozole, Anastrozole, or Exemestane Wit

rubric.target_patient: Women 18+ with histologically confirmed, node-positive (1-3 nodes) invasive breast carcinoma that is HR-positive (ER and/or PR ≥1%) and HER2-negative by ASCO/CAP criteria, with a commercial Oncotype DX Recurrence Score of 25 or less, post definitive breast surgery, without inflammatory or metastatic disease. This is a common early-stage, node-positive HR+/HER2- breast cancer population seen in general breast oncology practices, not a rare or biomarker-ultra-selected cohort.
rubric.site_burden: Based on the posted SWOG S1007 study calendar (weak evidence — heading-only match, not a structured visit grid, per the operational burden note), the trial requires: baseline history/physical with breast exam, weight/performance status, disease assessment, and HER2/hormone receptor confirmation prior to registration; central submission of tumor tissue for Oncotype DX assay (if not already done) and blood/tissue for correlative science specimens after Step 2 consent but before treatment start; assignment to chemotherapy-then-endocrine or endocrine-alone arms with site-determined visit frequency for labs/toxicity during chemotherapy; annual mammography for patients with an intact breast; and long-term structured follow-up for recurrence/survival every 3 months in year 1, every 6 months years 2-5, then annually to 15 years. This implies the site must support long-horizon longitudinal follow-up (up to 15 years), central specimen shipping logistics (Genomic Health, correlative science kits), coordination with a treating surgeon/pathologist for eligibility confirmation, and multi-year regulatory/data submission via SWOG mechanisms — but the exact visit-by-visit burden could not be confirmed from the excerpt provided.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a practicing breast oncology specialist (medical oncologist, surgical oncologist, or hematologist-oncologist with a breast focus) capable of managing HR+/HER2- adjuvant treatment decisions.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists the physician under Medical Oncology, Hematology & Oncology, or Surgical Oncology with an active practice location.
criteria.derived_from: Population: node-positive invasive breast carcinoma requiring adjuvant endocrine +/- chemotherapy decision-making

criteria.requirement: Investigator must be fluent in ASCO/CAP 2013 HER2 and hormone receptor testing guidelines to correctly interpret local pathology and confirm eligibility.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-attestation of routine use of ASCO/CAP HER2/HR testing algorithms in practice, or documented affiliation with a CAP-accredited pathology lab used for these determinations.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion criterion: ER/PR and HER2 status must be assessed per ASCO/CAP 2013 guidelines with specific IHC/ISH thresholds

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have working knowledge of Oncotype DX Recurrence Score interpretation and its role in adjuvant treatment decisions.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report of prior clinical use of Oncotype DX testing in adjuvant treatment planning, or PubMed-indexed authorship on genomic assay-guided breast cancer treatment.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion criterion requiring Recurrence Score of 25 or less via Oncotype DX Breast Cancer Assay

criteria.requirement: The practice must regularly see and surgically/medically manage node-positive (1-3 nodes), HR+/HER2- invasive breast cancer patients in sufficient volume to recruit into this trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-reported estimate of >=5 newly diagnosed node-positive HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients per year, or Medicare claims showing a floor of breast cancer-related E&M/chemotherapy administration visits consistent with an active breast oncology caseload.
criteria.derived_from: Eligibility: node positive (1-3 nodes), ER/PR+, HER2- invasive breast carcinoma

criteria.requirement: The site must have a referral pathway from breast surgeons within the 84-day post-definitive-surgery registration window, since eligibility requires prompt Step 2 registration after surgery.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report of an established referral relationship or shared-care arrangement with breast surgical oncology within the same institution or network.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: registration for Oncotype DX no later than 56 days, and Step 2 registration within 84 days after definitive surgery

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to maintain structured patient follow-up for recurrence/survival endpoints for up to 15 years post-randomization.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-attestation that the site has an active long-term oncology follow-up/tracking system (e.g., tumor registry or dedicated research coordinator) capable of sustaining multi-year protocol-mandated visit schedules.
criteria.derived_from: Study calendar: follow-up every 3 months year 1, every 6 months years 2-5, annually to 15 years after randomization

criteria.requirement: Site must have capability to collect, process, and ship tumor tissue and blood specimens to central labs (Genomic Health for Oncotype DX, correlative science repository) within protocol-specified windows.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report of existing specimen processing/shipping infrastructure (e.g., pathology block retrieval, blood draw and cold-chain shipping) used in prior cooperative group trials.
criteria.derived_from: Specimens section: tissue block/unstained sections sent to Genomic Health; correlative tissue/blood collected after Step 2 consent, prior to treatment

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to administer and monitor IV systemic chemotherapy regimens alongside oral endocrine therapy (tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors) for enrolled patients randomized to Arm 1.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report of on-site or affiliated infusion center capable of administering standard adjuvant chemotherapy regimens with toxicity monitoring.
criteria.derived_from: Interventions: Systemic Chemotherapy given IV; Treatment arms - chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy vs endocrine therapy alone

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience serving as investigator on NCI cooperative group (e.g., SWOG, NRG, ECOG-ACRIN) oncology trials, given this is an NCI/SWOG-sponsored phase III cooperative group study with complex multi-step registration.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician or their site as a participating investigator/site on >=1 other NCI cooperative group-sponsored oncology trial.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: National Cancer Institute (NCI); protocol identifier S1007 (SWOG); multi-step Step 1/Step 2/Step 3 registration structure

criteria.requirement: Investigator's site should demonstrate a track record of GCP-compliant conduct on industry- or NIH-sponsored interventional trials, given the trial's long follow-up and data submission requirements per SWOG guidelines.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research-related payments to the physician or affiliated institution from pharmaceutical/device sponsors, or ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as PI/sub-I on completed interventional trials with results reported.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: SWOG Best Practices document, data submission guidelines (Section 14.0), and multi-year protocol compliance requirements

excluded_screening: Histologic confirmation of node-positive HR+/HER2- status per ASCO/CAP thresholds — a per-patient pathology determination, not a site attribute
Oncotype DX Recurrence Score "25 or less" — a per-patient lab result gating eligibility, not investigator fitness
Timing windows for Oncotype DX submission (56 days) and Step 2 registration (84 days after surgery) — administrative per-patient deadlines
Complete history and physical within 28 days prior to registration — routine per-patient screening exam
Zubrod performance status 0-2 — per-patient functional status assessment
Informed consent (Step 1/Step 2 forms) — per-patient regulatory requirement, not a site qualification
Pregnancy/contraception requirements — per-patient reproductive status screening
Exclusion of inflammatory or metastatic breast cancer — per-patient diagnostic exclusion criterion
Synchronous bilateral disease and prior contralateral DCIS eligibility nuances — per-patient disease history adjudication, not physician capability
